SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeAdapter\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Bridge-agnostic adapter interface used by Master / Remote strategies. The adapter\n *         encapsulates one bridge transport (CCIP, CCTP, OP Stack canonical bridge) so the\n *         strategy stays bridge-ignorant. The adapter owns the envelope shape; the strategy\n *         only ever passes its own opaque `payload` bytes.\n *\n *         A single adapter deployment serves all authorised strategies on its chain, with\n *         per-sender lane configuration held inside the adapter. Each adapter is bound to\n *         one peer chain through the lane configuration.\n */\ninterface IBridgeAdapter {\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a message-only payload to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Strategy passes opaque `payload`; the adapter wraps `(msg.sender, payload)` into\n     *         its transport envelope. For bridges that require a native fee, caller must\n     *         supply `msg.value >= quoteFee.fee` (the adapter checks). No refund of excess \u2014\n     *         overpayment stays on the adapter and can be recovered by governor via\n     *         `transferToken(address(0), ...)`.\n     */\n    function sendMessage(bytes calldata payload) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a token transfer alongside a message to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Adapter pulls `amount` of `token` from `msg.sender` via `safeTransferFrom`,\n     *         then forwards via its bridge transport together with the wrapped envelope.\n     *         Same `msg.value` semantics as `sendMessage`.\n     */\n    function sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Quote the fee for the operation described by `(token, amount, payload)`.\n     *\n     * @return fee The fee amount, denominated in `feeToken`. When `feeToken == address(0)`\n     *             this is a native (ETH) fee. When non-zero this is an ERC20 fee in that\n     *             token. When the bridge protocol auto-deducts from the bridged amount\n     *             (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality), `fee` is informational only \u2014 the actual\n     *             deduction happens transparently inside the bridge, NOT on the caller.\n     * @return feeToken The token the fee is denominated in. `address(0)` means native.\n     * @return requiresExternalPayment True if the caller must supply `fee` of `feeToken`\n     *             alongside the send call (e.g., via `msg.value` for native). False if the\n     *             bridge protocol handles the fee transparently (e.g., deducts from the\n     *             bridged token amount). The strategy reads this flag to decide whether to\n     *             enforce a `msg.value` check; if false, the caller can ignore `fee`\n     *             entirely.\n     *\n     *         The three-value return separates two orthogonal concerns:\n     *           1. Pre-send caller action (do I need to pay anything separately?)\n     *           2. Post-send accounting (the actual deduction is surfaced via\n     *              `IBridgeReceiver.receiveMessage(... uint256 feePaid)` on the receiving\n     *              side, independent of this quote).\n     */\n    function quoteFee(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    )\n        external\n        view\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        );\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Per-tx maximum token amount this adapter accepts on outbound, and the implied\n     *         maximum it can deliver on inbound (since outbound and inbound are configured\n     *         as mirror sides of the same protocol lane).\n     *\n     *         Strategies use this in two places:\n     *           - Master.depositAll caps the locally-staged balance by\n     *             `outboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()` before sending.\n     *           - Master.withdrawAll caps the requested amount by\n     *             `inboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()`, because Master can't query Remote's\n     *             outbound across chains \u2014 the symmetric inbound adapter holds the same\n     *             protocol-level cap.\n     *\n     *         `0` means \"no enforcement\" (unlimited). Concrete adapters layer additional\n     *         protocol-level constants on top (e.g., CCTPAdapter enforces a hard 10M USDC\n     *         cap regardless of the configured value).\n     */\n    function maxTransferAmount() external view returns (uint256);\n}\n","keccak256":"0xc212efb34e7ad260919d44f2fd6c45507b96a378026e2c29c69e1df5d8423a03","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeReceiver\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Receiver hook implemented by Master and Remote strategies. The configured inbound\n *         adapter forwards incoming bridge deliveries through this single entry point.\n *\n *         The adapter MUST have transferred any inbound tokens to the strategy before\n *         invoking this function. The `amountReceived` argument carries the actual landed\n *         amount (post any transport-side fee deduction); the strategy accounts on\n *         `amountReceived` and may use `feePaid` for telemetry or sanity checks against the\n *         sender's intended amount carried inside `payload`.\n */\ninterface IBridgeReceiver {\n    /**\n     * @notice Called by the authorised inbound adapter when a message lands.\n     * @param sender         Strategy address on the source chain \u2014 under CREATE3 parity, the\n     *                       same address as the destination strategy on this chain.\n     * @param token          Token delivered alongside the message; `address(0)` for\n     *                       message-only deliveries.\n     * @param amountReceived Actual amount of `token` transferred to this strategy by the\n     *                       adapter immediately before this call (already received).\n     * @param feePaid        Transport-side fee deducted from the sender's intended amount\n     *                       (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality fee). Informational; the strategy's\n     *                       accounting is on `amountReceived`.\n     * @param payload        Strategy-owned opaque bytes from the source envelope.\n     */\n    function receiveMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x9a33146f1323765e9bf73470a5e1bf3e7e3c34a08f9e360cdd43f8c140cf1979","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport { SafeERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\";\n\nimport { Governable } from \"../../governance/Governable.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeAdapter } from \"../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeAdapter.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeReceiver } from \"../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Shared base for OUSD V3 Master (L2) and Remote (Ethereum) strategies. Provides:\n *           - Bridge-agnostic outbound / inbound adapter wiring.\n *           - Yield-channel nonce machinery (one yield op in flight at a time).\n *           - Inbound `receiveMessage` entry point with adapter-only access control,\n *             dispatching to a single hook the concrete strategy implements.\n *           - Outbound helpers (`_sendYieldMessage`, `_sendYieldTokensAndMessage`,\n *             `_sendMessage`) that pack `(msgType, nonce, body)` into the strategy-owned\n *             payload, quote the adapter fee, forward exact native via `msg.value`, and\n *             refund any excess back to the caller (user / operator).\n *\n *         The abstract does NOT itself inherit `InitializableAbstractStrategy` \u2014 it stays\n *         small and composable. Concrete Master / Remote contracts mix in\n *         `InitializableAbstractStrategy` separately.\n */\nabstract contract AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy is Governable, IBridgeReceiver {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event OutboundAdapterUpdated(address oldAdapter, address newAdapter);\n    event InboundAdapterUpdated(address oldAdapter, address newAdapter);\n    event OperatorUpdated(address oldOperator, address newOperator);\n    event YieldNonceAdvanced(uint64 nonce);\n    event YieldNonceProcessed(uint64 nonce);\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots; nothing relocated from any parent) ---------\n\n    /// @notice Adapter used to send outbound messages and tokens to the peer chain.\n    address public outboundAdapter;\n\n    /// @notice Adapter authorised to call `receiveMessage` on this strategy.\n    ///         For atomic bridges the outbound and inbound adapters can be the same address;\n    ///         for split-delivery they're typically different.\n    address public inboundAdapter;\n\n    /// @notice Account allowed to drive periodic, permissioned operations\n    ///         (balance check, settlement, claim trigger). Set by governor.\n    address public operator;\n\n    /// @notice Highest yield-channel nonce ever assigned.\n    uint64 public lastYieldNonce;\n\n    /// @notice Marks each yield-channel nonce as processed (true) once its\n    ///         message round-trip completes.\n    mapping(uint64 => bool) public nonceProcessed;\n\n    /// @notice Timestamp echoed back from the most-recently-accepted balance check ack.\n    ///         Used by `_processBalanceCheckResponse` to enforce strict monotonic ordering\n    ///         when multiple balance checks are in flight at the same yield-nonce window\n    ///         and responses can arrive out of order (CCIP delivery isn't FIFO).\n    // slither-disable-next-line constable-states\n    uint256 public lastBalanceCheckTimestamp;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion of this abstract layer.\n    uint256[43] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Modifiers ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    modifier onlyInboundAdapter() {\n        require(\n            inboundAdapter != address(0) && msg.sender == inboundAdapter,\n            \"V3: only inbound adapter\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    // --- Adapter / operator configuration (governor) ------------------------\n\n    function setOutboundAdapter(address _outboundAdapter)\n        external\n        onlyGovernor\n    {\n        _setOutboundAdapter(_outboundAdapter);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Hook for concrete strategies that need to perform token-allowance swaps when\n     *      the outbound adapter changes (e.g., revoke an old adapter's bridgeAsset\n     *      allowance, grant the new one max). Default implementation just rotates the\n     *      stored address; override to add side effects.\n     */\n    function _setOutboundAdapter(address _outboundAdapter) internal virtual {\n        emit OutboundAdapterUpdated(outboundAdapter, _outboundAdapter);\n        outboundAdapter = _outboundAdapter;\n    }\n\n    function setInboundAdapter(address _inboundAdapter) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit InboundAdapterUpdated(inboundAdapter, _inboundAdapter);\n        inboundAdapter = _inboundAdapter;\n    }\n\n    function setOperator(address _operator) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit OperatorUpdated(operator, _operator);\n        operator = _operator;\n    }\n\n    // --- Yield-channel nonce machinery --------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev True when a yield-channel operation has been initiated but its ack\n     *      has not yet been processed.\n     */\n    function isYieldOpInFlight() public view returns (bool) {\n        uint64 n = lastYieldNonce;\n        if (n == 0) return false;\n        return !nonceProcessed[n];\n    }\n\n    function _getNextYieldNonce() internal returns (uint64) {\n        require(!isYieldOpInFlight(), \"V3: yield op already in flight\");\n        lastYieldNonce += 1;\n        emit YieldNonceAdvanced(lastYieldNonce);\n        return lastYieldNonce;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by the initiating side (typically Master) when an ack lands. Requires the\n     *      nonce to match the most recently issued one and not yet be marked processed.\n     */\n    function _markYieldNonceProcessed(uint64 nonce) internal {\n        require(nonce == lastYieldNonce, \"V3: stale or unknown nonce\");\n        require(!nonceProcessed[nonce], \"V3: nonce already processed\");\n        nonceProcessed[nonce] = true;\n        emit YieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by the receiving side (typically Remote) when an inbound yield-channel\n     *      message arrives. The receiver doesn't issue nonces of its own; it adopts the\n     *      sender's nonce, enforcing strict monotonicity and one-time processing.\n     */\n    function _acceptYieldNonce(uint64 nonce) internal {\n        require(nonce > lastYieldNonce, \"V3: nonce not monotonic\");\n        require(!nonceProcessed[nonce], \"V3: nonce already processed\");\n        lastYieldNonce = nonce;\n        nonceProcessed[nonce] = true;\n        emit YieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n    }\n\n    // --- Inbound dispatch ---------------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @inheritdoc IBridgeReceiver\n     * @dev Single ingress for all inbound bridge deliveries. Validates the caller is the\n     *      configured inbound adapter, decodes the strategy-owned `(msgType, nonce, body)`\n     *      from `payload`, and forwards to the concrete strategy's hook. No `nonReentrant`\n     *      here \u2014 the concrete strategy's hook is the right place to apply it.\n     */\n    function receiveMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external override onlyInboundAdapter {\n        (uint32 msgType, uint64 nonce, bytes memory body) = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .unpackPayload(payload);\n        _handleBridgeMessage(\n            sender,\n            token,\n            amountReceived,\n            feePaid,\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Concrete strategies (Master / Remote) override this to dispatch by `msgType` and\n     *      implement the per-message logic. `body` is the message-specific payload (e.g.,\n     *      `abi.encode(newBalance)` for DEPOSIT_ACK).\n     */\n    function _handleBridgeMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal virtual;\n\n    // --- Outbound helpers ---------------------------------------------------\n    //\n    // Two parallel fee paths, distinguished by who pays:\n    //\n    //   _sendUserMessage / _sendUserTokensAndMessage\n    //     User-initiated sends (bridgeOTokenToPeer). msg.value MUST cover the fee.\n    //     No fallback to the strategy's pool. Rationale: an attacker could otherwise drain\n    //     the operator-funded pool by spamming bridge_in/out with msg.value=0. User pays\n    //     for their own bridge tx.\n    //\n    //   _sendOpMessage / _sendOpTokensAndMessage\n    //     Operator/protocol-funded sends (yield channel deposits/withdraws/claims and the\n    //     acks Remote sends in response to inbound). msg.value (if any) lands in\n    //     `address(this).balance` first via `receive()`; we then check `balance >= fee`,\n    //     which covers both pre-funded pool AND any msg.value attached.\n    //\n    // Excess msg.value is NEVER refunded. Overpayment becomes part of the strategy's pool.\n    // Recovery via `transferNative` (governor only). Rationale: refunds add code surface;\n    // callers can quote exactly via `IBridgeAdapter.quoteFee` to avoid leaks.\n\n    /// @dev Operator-funded yield-channel message send (message-only).\n    function _sendYieldMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendOpMessage(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Operator-funded yield-channel send carrying tokens (DEPOSIT, WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK).\n    function _sendYieldTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendOpTokensAndMessage(token, amount, msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev User-funded bridge-channel send (BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT). msg.value required.\n    function _sendBridgeMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendUserMessage(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Strict user-payment path: msg.value MUST cover fee. Pool is NOT consulted \u2014\n    ///      even if it has funds, a short user payment reverts. This is the security gate\n    ///      that prevents bridge_in/out from being a pool-drain vector.\n    function _sendUserMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(address(0), 0, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            // Only native fee supported today. ERC20 fee tokens (e.g., LINK-mode CCIP)\n            // would need explicit allowance handling; not implemented here. Forces any\n            // future fee-token addition to be an explicit override.\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"V3: insufficient user fee\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage{ value: fee }(payload);\n        } else {\n            // CCTP-style: protocol auto-deducts from bridged amount; no caller action.\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage(payload);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Pool-funded path: native fee paid from `address(this).balance`. msg.value (if\n    ///      any) already lands in balance via `receive()`, so this naturally covers both\n    ///      pre-funded pool AND inline operator top-ups.\n    function _sendOpMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(address(0), 0, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(address(this).balance >= fee, \"V3: pool unfunded\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage{ value: fee }(payload);\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage(payload);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Token-carrying variant of `_sendUserMessage`. Unused for V3 today (bridge\n    ///      channel is message-only on the wire), but kept symmetric for future use.\n    function _sendUserTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(token, amount, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"V3: insufficient user fee\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens{ value: fee }(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Token-carrying variant of `_sendOpMessage`. Used by DEPOSIT (Master) and\n    ///      WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK with tokens (Remote).\n    function _sendOpTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(token, amount, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(address(this).balance >= fee, \"V3: pool unfunded\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens{ value: fee }(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Sweep native ETH out of the strategy to governor. Used to drain the fee\n    ///         pool (operator rotation, decommission) or recover stray donations (a user\n    ///         that overpaid msg.value when calling `bridgeOTokenToPeer`).\n    function transferNative(uint256 amount) external onlyGovernor {\n        // slither-disable-next-line low-level-calls\n        (bool ok, ) = governor().call{ value: amount }(\"\");\n        require(ok, \"V3: native sweep failed\");\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Strategy accepts native ETH unconditionally. Lands in `address(this).balance`\n    ///      and serves as the fee pool. NEVER counted toward `checkBalance` \u2014 that function\n    ///      only sums bridge-asset-denominated slots, so ETH on this contract is naturally\n    ///      invisible to the L2 vault's accounting. (No explicit \"exclude ETH\" code needed.)\n    receive() external payable virtual {}\n}\n","keccak256":"0xc38346304bb6535cf44652314e570d138b1a499619ed5e77cf1f249c00c1175a","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/AbstractWOTokenStrategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20, SafeERC20, InitializableAbstractStrategy } from \"../../utils/InitializableAbstractStrategy.sol\";\nimport { IVault } from \"../../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\n\nimport { AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy } from \"./AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Shared base for the wOToken cross-chain strategy pair (Master on L2, Remote on\n *         Ethereum). Lifts everything that's duplicated between Master and Remote:\n *\n *           - Constants + immutables (bridgeAsset, oToken, MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS).\n *           - Bridge-channel state (bridgeAdjustment, consumedBridgeIds, bridgeIdCounter).\n *           - Generic bridge-channel mechanics: outbound send (`bridgeOTokenToPeer`),\n *             inbound dispatch (`_handleInboundBridgeMessage`), replay protection, signed\n *             `bridgeAdjustment` bookkeeping, optional post-delivery callback.\n *           - `_abstractSetPToken` and `collectRewardTokens` no-op stubs (Strategy base\n *             requires them; neither strategy uses them).\n *           - `onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist` modifier (operator OR strategist OR governor).\n *\n *         Concrete strategies implement four hooks for the small middle of each bridge op\n *         that differs between the two sides:\n *\n *           - `_bridgeOutboundMsgType()` \u2014 Master: BRIDGE_OUT, Remote: BRIDGE_IN.\n *           - `_preflightBridgeOutbound(amount)` \u2014 Master: liquidity check, Remote: no-op.\n *           - `_consumeOTokenForBridge(amount)` \u2014 Master: burn via vault, Remote: wrap to wOToken.\n *           - `_deliverOTokenForBridge(amount, recipient)` \u2014 Master: mint+transfer, Remote: unwrap+transfer.\n */\nabstract contract AbstractWOTokenStrategy is\n    AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy,\n    InitializableAbstractStrategy\n{\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Constants & immutables --------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Maximum gas forwarded to the optional post-delivery `callData` call on\n    ///         the bridge channel. Caps griefing surface; users can request lower per call.\n    uint32 public constant MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS = 500000;\n\n    /// @notice Maximum protocol fee on the bridge channel (10% in basis points).\n    uint256 public constant MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS = 1000;\n\n    /// @notice Asset that bridges between Master and Remote (USDC for OUSD V3, WETH for OETHb).\n    address public immutable bridgeAsset;\n\n    /// @notice OToken on this chain (the rebasing OToken \u2014 OUSD, OETH, OETHb, etc.).\n    address public immutable oToken;\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots) -------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Signed net delta from bridge-channel activity since the last settlement.\n    ///         BRIDGE_IN (mint locally / wrap locally) \u2192 increases.\n    ///         BRIDGE_OUT (burn locally / unwrap locally) \u2192 decreases.\n    int256 public bridgeAdjustment;\n\n    /// @notice Replay protection for the nonceless bridge channel.\n    mapping(bytes32 => bool) public consumedBridgeIds;\n\n    /// @notice Monotonic counter used to generate fresh bridgeIds for outbound BRIDGE_IN\n    ///         / BRIDGE_OUT operations. Combined with `address(this)` for global uniqueness.\n    uint256 public bridgeIdCounter;\n\n    /// @notice Protocol fee on the bridge channel in basis points (1 bp = 0.01%). Default\n    ///         0; capped at `MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS`. When > 0, the source side consumes the\n    ///         full `_amount` of OToken while the envelope carries `net = _amount - fee`,\n    ///         so the peer only delivers `net`. The retained `fee` worth of backing flows\n    ///         through the next `BALANCE_CHECK` and lifts the vault's rebase by the\n    ///         fee.\n    uint256 public bridgeFeeBps;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion of this abstract layer.\n    uint256[43] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event BridgeRequested(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed sender,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 fee,\n        bytes callData,\n        uint32 callGasLimit\n    );\n    event BridgeDelivered(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount\n    );\n    event BridgeFeeBpsUpdated(uint256 oldBps, uint256 newBps);\n    event BridgeCallSucceeded(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount\n    );\n    event BridgeCallFailed(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes returnData\n    );\n\n    // --- Construction -------------------------------------------------------\n\n    constructor(\n        BaseStrategyConfig memory _stratConfig,\n        address _bridgeAsset,\n        address _oToken\n    ) InitializableAbstractStrategy(_stratConfig) {\n        require(_bridgeAsset != address(0), \"WOT: bridge asset required\");\n        require(_oToken != address(0), \"WOT: oToken required\");\n        bridgeAsset = _bridgeAsset;\n        oToken = _oToken;\n    }\n\n    // --- Modifiers ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Permits the operator, strategist, or governor.\n    modifier onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist() {\n        require(\n            msg.sender == operator ||\n                isGovernor() ||\n                msg.sender == IVault(vaultAddress).strategistAddr(),\n            \"WOT: not authorised\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    // --- Strategy-base shims (no-op) ---------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function supportsAsset(address _asset) public view override returns (bool) {\n        return _asset == bridgeAsset;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function _abstractSetPToken(address, address) internal override {}\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function collectRewardTokens()\n        external\n        override\n        onlyHarvesterOrStrategist\n        nonReentrant\n    {}\n\n    // --- Bridge channel: outbound -------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @notice User-initiated bridge: burn (Master) or wrap (Remote) `_amount` of OToken\n     *         locally and instruct the peer chain to deliver the equivalent amount.\n     * @param _amount        OToken amount to bridge.\n     * @param _recipient     Destination on the peer chain. `address(0)` defaults to msg.sender.\n     * @param _callData      Optional calldata invoked on `_recipient` after token delivery on\n     *                       the destination side. Empty for plain bridge.\n     * @param _callGasLimit  Per-call gas cap; must be \u2264 MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS.\n     */\n    function bridgeOTokenToPeer(\n        uint256 _amount,\n        address _recipient,\n        bytes calldata _callData,\n        uint32 _callGasLimit\n    ) external payable nonReentrant {\n        require(_amount > 0, \"WOT: zero bridge\");\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"WOT: outbound not set\");\n        require(\n            _callGasLimit <= MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS,\n            \"WOT: callGasLimit too high\"\n        );\n        require(\n            _callData.length == 0 || _callGasLimit > 0,\n            \"WOT: callData needs gas\"\n        );\n        _bridgeOutboundExec(_amount, _recipient, _callData, _callGasLimit);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Split from `bridgeOTokenToPeer` to keep the public function's stack within\n    ///      limits \u2014 the burn-full/deliver-net + envelope build chain pushes several\n    ///      locals that crowd the verifier.\n    function _bridgeOutboundExec(\n        uint256 _amount,\n        address _recipient,\n        bytes calldata _callData,\n        uint32 _callGasLimit\n    ) private {\n        // Burn-full / deliver-net: protocol fee is consumed on the source (full `_amount`)\n        // but only `net` flows across the bridge; the difference is the retained backing\n        // that lifts the next rebase.\n        uint256 fee = (_amount * bridgeFeeBps) / 10000;\n        uint256 net = _amount - fee;\n        require(net > 0, \"WOT: net zero after fee\");\n\n        // Pre-flight against `net` (what the peer must produce).\n        _preflightBridgeOutbound(net);\n\n        address recipient = _recipient == address(0) ? msg.sender : _recipient;\n\n        // Master burns FULL `_amount`; Remote wraps FULL `_amount`. The fee portion stays\n        // as backing on the wOToken side and accrues to yield via the next BALANCE_CHECK.\n        _consumeOTokenForBridge(_amount);\n\n        uint32 msgType = _bridgeOutboundMsgType();\n        // Accounting captures the obligation that's actually leaving \u2014 `net`.\n        _applyBridgeAdjustment(msgType, net);\n\n        bytes32 bridgeId = _nextBridgeId();\n        bytes memory body = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeBridgeUserPayload(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload({\n                bridgeId: bridgeId,\n                amount: net,\n                recipient: recipient,\n                callData: _callData,\n                callGasLimit: _callGasLimit\n            })\n        );\n        _sendBridgeMessage(msgType, 0, body);\n\n        emit BridgeRequested(\n            bridgeId,\n            msg.sender,\n            recipient,\n            net,\n            fee,\n            _callData,\n            _callGasLimit\n        );\n    }\n\n    // --- Governance --------------------------------------------------------\n\n    function setBridgeFeeBps(uint256 _bps) external onlyGovernor {\n        require(_bps <= MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS, \"WOT: fee too high\");\n        emit BridgeFeeBpsUpdated(bridgeFeeBps, _bps);\n        bridgeFeeBps = _bps;\n    }\n\n    // --- Bridge channel: inbound -------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by concrete strategies from `_handleBridgeMessage` when an inbound\n     *      BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT envelope arrives. Replay-checked, applies signed\n     *      `bridgeAdjustment`, invokes the side-specific delivery hook, runs the optional\n     *      post-delivery callback.\n     */\n    function _handleInboundBridgeMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload memory p = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .decodeBridgeUserPayload(body);\n\n        require(!consumedBridgeIds[p.bridgeId], \"WOT: bridgeId replayed\");\n        // Bridge-channel messages are message-only by design; tokens never ride along.\n        require(amount == 0, \"WOT: bridge-in tokens not expected\");\n        require(\n            p.callGasLimit <= MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS,\n            \"WOT: callGasLimit too high\"\n        );\n\n        // CEI: mark consumed, update accounting, deliver tokens, optional call.\n        consumedBridgeIds[p.bridgeId] = true;\n        _applyBridgeAdjustment(msgType, p.amount);\n\n        // Side-specific delivery (Master: mint + transfer; Remote: unwrap + transfer).\n        _deliverOTokenForBridge(p.amount, p.recipient);\n\n        emit BridgeDelivered(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount);\n\n        if (p.callData.length == 0) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        _postDeliveryCall(p);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Best-effort post-delivery call on the recipient. Never reverts; tokens have\n     *      already been delivered before this runs. No msg.value forwarded; gas bounded\n     *      by `p.callGasLimit` (already capped above by MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS).\n     */\n    function _postDeliveryCall(CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload memory p)\n        private\n    {\n        // slither-disable-next-line low-level-calls,unchecked-lowlevel\n        (bool ok, bytes memory ret) = p.recipient.call{\n            value: 0,\n            gas: p.callGasLimit\n        }(p.callData);\n        if (ok) {\n            emit BridgeCallSucceeded(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount);\n        } else {\n            emit BridgeCallFailed(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount, ret);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Apply the signed delta to `bridgeAdjustment` based on the message type. Both\n     *      Master and Remote use the same convention: BRIDGE_IN increases (mint/wrap),\n     *      BRIDGE_OUT decreases (burn/unwrap). The sign is determined by the message type\n     *      alone \u2014 no per-side configuration needed.\n     */\n    function _applyBridgeAdjustment(uint32 msgType, uint256 amount) internal {\n        if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_IN) {\n            bridgeAdjustment += int256(amount);\n        } else {\n            // BRIDGE_OUT (only other valid bridge-channel type; caller enforces this).\n            bridgeAdjustment -= int256(amount);\n        }\n    }\n\n    function _nextBridgeId() internal returns (bytes32) {\n        bridgeIdCounter += 1;\n        return keccak256(abi.encode(address(this), bridgeIdCounter));\n    }\n\n    // --- Hooks (concrete strategies implement) -----------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Outbound bridge-channel message type. Master: BRIDGE_OUT, Remote: BRIDGE_IN.\n    function _bridgeOutboundMsgType() internal pure virtual returns (uint32);\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Side-specific pre-flight check before consuming OToken on outbound bridge.\n     *         Master: ensures Remote has reported (or expects via bridgeAdjustment) enough\n     *         OToken to cover the delivery. Remote: no-op (wrapping always succeeds when\n     *         the user supplies the OToken).\n     */\n    function _preflightBridgeOutbound(uint256 amount) internal view virtual;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Pull OToken from `msg.sender` and consume it on this chain.\n     *         Master: burn via the OToken vault. Remote: wrap to wOToken via the ERC-4626.\n     */\n    function _consumeOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount) internal virtual;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Produce OToken on this chain and deliver it to `recipient`.\n     *         Master: mint via the OToken vault, then transfer. Remote: unwrap wOToken to\n     *         OToken, then transfer.\n     */\n    function _deliverOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount, address recipient)\n        internal\n        virtual;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x33f242be6183f4aa3572888eb52b35cffc3811b8b34a48e6e21023aa1635ad50","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/CrossChainV3Helper.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title CrossChainV3Helper\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @dev Strategy-level message-type constants and payload codecs for OUSD V3\n *      cross-chain messages. The wire envelope (sender + intendedAmount + payload) is\n *      bridge-adapter-internal; strategies only encode and decode the per-message-type\n *      payloads below, with the message type discriminator embedded inside the payload\n *      itself.\n */\nlibrary CrossChainV3Helper {\n    // --- Message type discriminators ---------------------------------------\n\n    // Yield channel (nonce-gated, one operation in flight at a time)\n\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: deposit `amount` of bridgeAsset (carried by the adapter).\n    uint32 internal constant DEPOSIT = 1;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: deposit acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant DEPOSIT_ACK = 2;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: leg-1 withdrawal request for `amount` of bridgeAsset.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_REQUEST = 3;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: leg-1 acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK = 4;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: leg-2 trigger to ship the previously-queued amount.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_CLAIM = 5;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: leg-2 ack carrying bridgeAsset on success.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK = 6;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: read Remote's balance snapshot at a given timestamp.\n    uint32 internal constant BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST = 7;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: balance response (balance + originating timestamp).\n    uint32 internal constant BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE = 8;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: clear the bridge-adjustment accounting on both sides.\n    uint32 internal constant SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING = 9;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: settlement acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK = 10;\n\n    // Bridge channel (nonceless, multiple operations in flight)\n\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: user-driven bridge of OToken from Ethereum onto the L2.\n    uint32 internal constant BRIDGE_IN = 11;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: user-driven bridge of OToken from L2 back to Ethereum.\n    uint32 internal constant BRIDGE_OUT = 12;\n\n    // --- Bridge user payload (BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT) -----------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev User-supplied payload for the bridge channel. Encoded inside the\n     *      envelope body. The destination strategy uses `bridgeId` for replay\n     *      protection (see Master / Remote `consumedBridgeIds` mapping) and\n     *      validates `callGasLimit` against its adapter-configured maximum\n     *      before issuing the optional post-delivery call.\n     */\n    struct BridgeUserPayload {\n        bytes32 bridgeId;\n        uint256 amount;\n        address recipient;\n        bytes callData;\n        uint32 callGasLimit;\n    }\n\n    // --- Strategy-level envelope (msgType + nonce + body) -------------------\n    //\n    // Strategies wrap their per-op body bytes inside a small strategy-owned envelope so a\n    // single `payload` field can carry message-type discrimination and a yield-channel\n    // nonce without leaking those concerns into the bridge adapter. The adapter sees the\n    // strategy envelope as opaque bytes.\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Build the strategy-level envelope: `abi.encode(msgType, nonce, body)`.\n     */\n    function packPayload(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Decode the strategy-level envelope.\n     */\n    function unpackPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            uint32 msgType,\n            uint64 nonce,\n            bytes memory body\n        )\n    {\n        (msgType, nonce, body) = abi.decode(payload, (uint32, uint64, bytes));\n    }\n\n    // --- Per-message payload encoders / decoders ----------------------------\n    //\n    // DEPOSIT                       : payload empty; amount is carried by the adapter\n    // DEPOSIT_ACK                   : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // WITHDRAW_REQUEST              : payload = abi.encode(amount)\n    // WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK          : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // WITHDRAW_CLAIM                : payload empty\n    // WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK            : payload = abi.encode(newBalance, success, amount)\n    // BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST         : payload = abi.encode(timestamp)\n    // BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE        : payload = abi.encode(balance, timestamp)\n    // SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING      : payload empty\n    // SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK  : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT        : payload = abi.encode(BridgeUserPayload)\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the single-uint256 payload used by DEPOSIT_ACK,\n     *         WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK, and SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK.\n     * @param newBalance Remote's `checkBalance(bridgeAsset)` snapshot after the op.\n     */\n    function encodeNewBalancePayload(uint256 newBalance)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the single-uint256 payload above.\n    function decodeNewBalancePayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 newBalance)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the WITHDRAW_REQUEST payload (the leg-1 amount Master wants).\n     * @param amount  bridgeAsset units to withdraw.\n     */\n    function encodeAmountPayload(uint256 amount)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the WITHDRAW_REQUEST payload.\n    function decodeAmountPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 amount)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK payload. The only R\u2192M yield message that\n     *         carries tokens \u2014 `amount` pins the exact bridgeAsset bundled with the\n     *         message (0 on NACK or message-only) so split-delivery receivers can set\n     *         `expectedAmount` without inspecting the bridge transport.\n     * @param newBalance Remote's `checkBalance` after the claim leg.\n     * @param success    `true` if the claim shipped tokens, `false` if leg-2 NACK'd.\n     * @param amount     bridgeAsset units bundled with this ack; 0 when `success` is false.\n     */\n    function encodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(\n        uint256 newBalance,\n        bool success,\n        uint256 amount\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(newBalance, success, amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK 3-tuple payload.\n    function decodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            uint256 newBalance,\n            bool success,\n            uint256 amount\n        )\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256, bool, uint256));\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Encode the BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST payload (origin timestamp).\n    function encodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(uint256 timestamp)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(timestamp);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST payload.\n    function decodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 timestamp)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Encode the BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE payload (balance + originating ts).\n    function encodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(\n        uint256 balance,\n        uint256 timestamp\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(balance, timestamp);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE 2-tuple payload.\n    function decodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 balance, uint256 timestamp)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256, uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT payload \u2014 packs the 5 user-supplied\n     *         fields the receiving strategy needs to deliver tokens and run the\n     *         optional post-delivery call.\n     */\n    function encodeBridgeUserPayload(BridgeUserPayload memory p)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return\n            abi.encode(\n                p.bridgeId,\n                p.amount,\n                p.recipient,\n                p.callData,\n                p.callGasLimit\n            );\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT payload into a `BridgeUserPayload`.\n    function decodeBridgeUserPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (BridgeUserPayload memory)\n    {\n        (\n            bytes32 bridgeId,\n            uint256 amount,\n            address recipient,\n            bytes memory callData,\n            uint32 callGasLimit\n        ) = abi.decode(payload, (bytes32, uint256, address, bytes, uint32));\n        return\n            BridgeUserPayload({\n                bridgeId: bridgeId,\n                amount: amount,\n                recipient: recipient,\n                callData: callData,\n                callGasLimit: callGasLimit\n            });\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x22ea4faf6d8b2ac9fb98665db7a03aed732282573bf3df0a876bf1eff2a20e02","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/MasterWOTokenStrategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20, SafeERC20, InitializableAbstractStrategy } from \"../../utils/InitializableAbstractStrategy.sol\";\nimport { IVault } from \"../../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeAdapter } from \"../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeAdapter.sol\";\n\nimport { AbstractWOTokenStrategy } from \"./AbstractWOTokenStrategy.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title MasterWOTokenStrategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Vault-facing leg of the wOToken cross-chain strategy pair. Registered with the\n *         OToken vault on its own chain; orchestrates deposits, withdrawals, balance\n *         checks, and settlement against the Remote strategy on the peer chain. Topology\n *         is deployment-dependent (e.g., for OETHb, Master is on Base and Remote on\n *         Ethereum; for OUSD V3, the topology can be inverted per spoke).\n *         Bridge-channel mechanics (`bridgeOTokenToPeer`,\n *         inbound BRIDGE_IN handling, replay protection, signed `bridgeAdjustment`\n *         bookkeeping) live in `AbstractWOTokenStrategy` and are wired here via four hooks.\n *\n *         Master is intentionally dumb on the withdrawal queue. It never sees a `requestId`,\n *         never tracks per-withdrawal state beyond a single in-flight amount flag \u2014 Remote\n *         owns the queue lifecycle. See the V3 design plan for the full state-transition table.\n */\ncontract MasterWOTokenStrategy is AbstractWOTokenStrategy {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots; nothing from any parent is relocated) -----\n\n    /// @notice Last reported Remote balance, denominated in `bridgeAsset` units.\n    ///         Updated by each yield-channel ack (deposit, withdrawal, balance check, settlement).\n    uint256 public remoteStrategyBalance;\n\n    /// @notice In-flight deposit amount (zero when no deposit is pending).\n    ///         Part of `checkBalance` so that bridged-but-not-yet-acked tokens stay accounted for.\n    uint256 public pendingAmount;\n\n    /// @notice In-flight withdrawal amount (zero when no withdrawal is pending). Pure state flag \u2014\n    ///         NOT part of `checkBalance` because the value is already covered by the stale\n    ///         `remoteStrategyBalance` until the leg-2 ack lands.\n    uint256 public pendingWithdrawalAmount;\n\n    /// @notice Snapshot of `bridgeAdjustment` captured at the moment `requestSettlement`\n    ///         fires. The ack handler subtracts exactly this value (not zero) so that any\n    ///         bridge ops processed between request and ack are preserved on both sides.\n    ///         See `_processSettlementAck` for rationale.\n    int256 public settlementSnapshot;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion.\n    uint256[41] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(uint256 newBalance);\n    event DepositRequested(uint64 nonce, uint256 amount);\n    event DepositAcked(uint64 nonce, uint256 newBalance);\n    event WithdrawRequested(uint64 nonce, uint256 amount);\n    event WithdrawRequestAcked(uint64 nonce, uint256 newBalance);\n    event WithdrawClaimTriggered(uint64 nonce, uint256 amount);\n    event WithdrawClaimAcked(uint64 nonce, uint256 newBalance, bool success);\n    event BalanceCheckRequested(uint64 nonce, uint256 timestamp);\n    event BalanceCheckResponded(\n        uint64 nonce,\n        uint256 newBalance,\n        uint256 remoteTimestamp\n    );\n    event SettlementRequested(uint64 nonce, int256 unsettledAtRequest);\n    event SettlementAcked(uint64 nonce, uint256 newBalance);\n\n    // --- Construction / initialisation -------------------------------------\n\n    constructor(\n        BaseStrategyConfig memory _stratConfig,\n        address _bridgeAsset,\n        address _oToken\n    ) AbstractWOTokenStrategy(_stratConfig, _bridgeAsset, _oToken) {\n        require(\n            _stratConfig.platformAddress == address(0),\n            \"Master: platform must be zero\"\n        );\n        require(\n            _stratConfig.vaultAddress != address(0),\n            \"Master: vault required\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    function initialize(address _operator) external onlyGovernor initializer {\n        operator = _operator;\n\n        address[] memory rewardTokens = new address[](0);\n        address[] memory assets = new address[](1);\n        address[] memory pTokens = new address[](1);\n        assets[0] = bridgeAsset;\n        pTokens[0] = bridgeAsset; // No pToken; mirror the bridgeAsset for the registry.\n\n        InitializableAbstractStrategy._initialize(\n            rewardTokens,\n            assets,\n            pTokens\n        );\n    }\n\n    // --- Required strategy overrides ---------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function checkBalance(address _asset)\n        external\n        view\n        override\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        require(_asset == bridgeAsset, \"Master: unsupported asset\");\n        // Local + in-flight deposit + last reported remote balance.\n        // pendingWithdrawalAmount is NOT included \u2014 value is already in remoteStrategyBalance\n        // until the leg-2 ack lands (see state-transition table in the design plan).\n        // bridgeAdjustment captures unsettled bridge-channel activity (signed).\n        int256 total = int256(\n            IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this)) +\n                pendingAmount +\n                remoteStrategyBalance\n        ) + bridgeAdjustment;\n        // Clamp to zero \u2014 bridgeAdjustment is bounded by burnForStrategy authorisation\n        // (can't be more negative than remoteStrategyBalance + previously settled bridge-in).\n        return total > 0 ? uint256(total) : 0;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function safeApproveAllTokens()\n        external\n        override\n        onlyGovernor\n        nonReentrant\n    {\n        // No platform to approve. Outbound adapter is approved on-demand in `_depositToRemote`.\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function deposit(address _asset, uint256 _amount)\n        external\n        override\n        onlyVault\n        nonReentrant\n    {\n        _depositToRemote(_asset, _amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    /// @dev Clamps the local balance by the outbound adapter's `maxTransferAmount` so a\n    ///      vault sweep larger than the bridge's per-tx limit lands as a partial deposit\n    ///      rather than reverting deep inside the bridge router. Remainder stays on Master\n    ///      until the next `depositAll` (or operator-driven sequencing).\n    function depositAll() external override onlyVault nonReentrant {\n        uint256 bal = IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this));\n        if (bal == 0) return;\n        uint256 cap = IBridgeAdapter(outboundAdapter).maxTransferAmount();\n        if (cap > 0 && bal > cap) bal = cap;\n        _depositToRemote(bridgeAsset, bal);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    /// @dev Withdrawals are async: this kicks off leg 1 (WITHDRAW_REQUEST). The actual\n    ///      tokens land later when `triggerClaim()` is invoked and the leg-2 ack returns.\n    ///      The `_recipient` parameter is informational \u2014 Master forwards received bridgeAsset\n    ///      to the vault on leg-2 ack regardless of this value.\n    function withdraw(\n        address _recipient,\n        address _asset,\n        uint256 _amount\n    ) external override onlyVault nonReentrant {\n        require(_recipient == vaultAddress, \"Master: recipient must be vault\");\n        _withdrawRequest(_asset, _amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    /// @dev Best-effort sweep: requests withdrawal of `remoteStrategyBalance` (clamped by\n    ///      Remote's per-tx bridge cap) if nothing else is in flight; otherwise silently\n    ///      no-ops so the vault sweep stays safe.\n    ///\n    ///      Clamping uses `inboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()` \u2014 Master can't query\n    ///      Remote's outbound across chains, but the symmetric inbound adapter on this\n    ///      chain holds the same protocol-level cap (outbound and inbound on a lane\n    ///      are mirror sides of the same bridge). For OETHb that's the Superbridge cap\n    ///      (canonical bridge, typically 0 = unlimited); for OUSD V3 it's the CCTPAdapter\n    ///      cap (10M USDC).\n    function withdrawAll() external override onlyVaultOrGovernor nonReentrant {\n        if (\n            pendingAmount != 0 ||\n            pendingWithdrawalAmount != 0 ||\n            isYieldOpInFlight()\n        ) {\n            return;\n        }\n        uint256 amount = remoteStrategyBalance;\n        if (amount == 0) return;\n        uint256 cap = IBridgeAdapter(inboundAdapter).maxTransferAmount();\n        if (cap > 0 && amount > cap) amount = cap;\n        _withdrawRequest(bridgeAsset, amount);\n    }\n\n    // --- Operator entrypoints ---------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Operator-triggered leg 2: instructs Remote to claim from its OToken-vault queue\n     *         (if not already done by peer-chain automation) and bridge the bridgeAsset back.\n     *         Must be called only after a leg-1 ack has been processed (otherwise no\n     *         pending withdrawal to claim).\n     */\n    function triggerClaim()\n        external\n        payable\n        nonReentrant\n        onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist\n    {\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"Master: outbound not set\");\n        require(pendingWithdrawalAmount > 0, \"Master: no pending withdrawal\");\n        require(!isYieldOpInFlight(), \"Master: yield op in flight\");\n\n        uint64 nonce = _getNextYieldNonce();\n        _sendYieldMessage(CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_CLAIM, nonce, \"\");\n\n        emit WithdrawClaimTriggered(nonce, pendingWithdrawalAmount);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Operator-triggered yield-channel round-trip to refresh `remoteStrategyBalance`\n     *         off the back of Remote's `previewRedeem`. Run on a cron (~2h) in production.\n     *\n     * @dev    Non-blocking: does NOT advance the yield nonce. Sends with the CURRENT\n     *         `lastYieldNonce` as an \"epoch marker\" \u2014 the response is accepted only if\n     *         that nonce still matches when the ack lands AND no other yield op is in\n     *         flight AND the timestamp is newer than the last accepted check. See\n     *         `_processBalanceCheckResponse` for the three-guard logic.\n     *\n     *         Multiple BCs in flight at the same nonce are harmless; whichever response\n     *         is newest wins via the timestamp guard.\n     */\n    function requestBalanceCheck()\n        external\n        payable\n        nonReentrant\n        onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist\n    {\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"Master: outbound not set\");\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .encodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(block.timestamp);\n        // Echo current nonce; do NOT advance it. Read-only on Remote's side.\n        _sendYieldMessage(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST,\n            lastYieldNonce,\n            payload\n        );\n        emit BalanceCheckRequested(lastYieldNonce, block.timestamp);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Operator-triggered settlement: zero out (or reduce) `bridgeAdjustment` on\n     *         both sides. With the locked design (yield-only baseline in balance check),\n     *         settlement is housekeeping \u2014 keeps bridgeAdjustment magnitude bounded\n     *         rather than being correctness-critical.\n     *\n     * @dev    Captures `bridgeAdjustment` as a snapshot at request time. Both sides\n     *         subtract exactly that snapshot on their respective handlers (NOT `= 0`),\n     *         which preserves any bridge ops that happen between request and ack. This\n     *         avoids the desync that would occur if both sides naively zeroed while a\n     *         new BRIDGE_OUT was mid-flight. See `_processSettlementAck` for the math.\n     */\n    function requestSettlement()\n        external\n        payable\n        nonReentrant\n        onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist\n    {\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"Master: outbound not set\");\n        require(!isYieldOpInFlight(), \"Master: yield op in flight\");\n        require(pendingWithdrawalAmount == 0, \"Master: withdrawal pending\");\n\n        uint64 nonce = _getNextYieldNonce();\n        // Persist for the ack handler to subtract from the (possibly-evolved) bridgeAdjustment.\n        settlementSnapshot = bridgeAdjustment;\n        bytes memory payload = abi.encode(settlementSnapshot);\n        _sendYieldMessage(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING,\n            nonce,\n            payload\n        );\n        emit SettlementRequested(nonce, settlementSnapshot);\n    }\n\n    // --- Yield channel: deposit --------------------------------------------\n\n    function _depositToRemote(address _asset, uint256 _amount) internal {\n        require(_asset == bridgeAsset, \"Master: unsupported asset\");\n        require(_amount > 0, \"Master: zero deposit\");\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"Master: outbound not set\");\n        require(\n            pendingAmount == 0 && pendingWithdrawalAmount == 0,\n            \"Master: yield op in flight\"\n        );\n\n        uint64 nonce = _getNextYieldNonce();\n        pendingAmount = _amount;\n\n        IERC20(bridgeAsset).safeApprove(outboundAdapter, _amount);\n        _sendYieldTokensAndMessage(\n            bridgeAsset,\n            _amount,\n            CrossChainV3Helper.DEPOSIT,\n            nonce,\n            \"\"\n        );\n\n        emit DepositRequested(nonce, _amount);\n        emit Deposit(bridgeAsset, bridgeAsset, _amount);\n    }\n\n    // --- Yield channel: withdrawal Option 1 (leg 1) ------------------------\n\n    function _withdrawRequest(address _asset, uint256 _amount) internal {\n        require(_asset == bridgeAsset, \"Master: unsupported asset\");\n        require(_amount > 0, \"Master: zero withdraw\");\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"Master: outbound not set\");\n        require(\n            pendingAmount == 0 && pendingWithdrawalAmount == 0,\n            \"Master: yield op in flight\"\n        );\n        require(\n            _amount <= remoteStrategyBalance,\n            \"Master: amount exceeds remote balance\"\n        );\n\n        uint64 nonce = _getNextYieldNonce();\n        pendingWithdrawalAmount = _amount;\n\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeAmountPayload(_amount);\n        _sendYieldMessage(CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_REQUEST, nonce, payload);\n\n        emit WithdrawRequested(nonce, _amount);\n    }\n\n    // --- Inbound dispatch --------------------------------------------------\n\n    function _handleBridgeMessage(\n        address, // sender\n        address, // token\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256, // feePaid \u2014 unused for bridge channel / yield message-only ops\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal override {\n        if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.DEPOSIT_ACK) {\n            _processYieldDepositAck(nonce, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK) {\n            _processWithdrawRequestAck(nonce, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK) {\n            _processWithdrawClaimAck(nonce, amountReceived, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_IN) {\n            _handleInboundBridgeMessage(msgType, amountReceived, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE) {\n            _processBalanceCheckResponse(nonce, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK) {\n            _processSettlementAck(nonce, body);\n        } else {\n            revert(\"Master: unsupported message type\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Three-guard acceptance:\n    ///        1. `!isYieldOpInFlight()` \u2014 if a deposit/withdraw is mid-flight, the response\n    ///           would race with its ack; ignore to avoid corrupting pendingAmount /\n    ///           remoteStrategyBalance accounting.\n    ///        2. `respNonce == lastYieldNonce` \u2014 the request was sent at this nonce; if\n    ///           lastYieldNonce has since advanced, this response is from a now-stale\n    ///           epoch. Ignore.\n    ///        3. `respTimestamp > lastBalanceCheckTimestamp` \u2014 out-of-order CCIP delivery\n    ///           could land an older snapshot after a newer one. Strict monotonic order\n    ///           preserves the latest read.\n    function _processBalanceCheckResponse(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        // No _markYieldNonceProcessed here \u2014 balance check did NOT advance the nonce, so\n        // there's nothing to mark. The 3 guards below replace nonce-advance semantics.\n        if (isYieldOpInFlight()) return;\n        if (nonce != lastYieldNonce) return;\n        (uint256 newBalance, uint256 remoteTimestamp) = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .decodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(payload);\n        if (remoteTimestamp <= lastBalanceCheckTimestamp) return;\n        lastBalanceCheckTimestamp = remoteTimestamp;\n        remoteStrategyBalance = newBalance;\n        emit BalanceCheckResponded(nonce, newBalance, remoteTimestamp);\n        emit RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Subtracts `settlementSnapshot` (NOT `= 0`). Rationale:\n    ///\n    ///        Master.bridgeAdj at ack time may differ from what it was at request time if\n    ///        new bridge ops landed in between. Zeroing would erase those new ops. By\n    ///        subtracting only the exact snapshot we committed to settling, we preserve\n    ///        the post-snapshot delta on both sides \u2014 Remote does the symmetric subtract\n    ///        in `_processSettlement`, so both sides converge to the same value\n    ///        regardless of the order in which bridge ops vs. the settle message reach\n    ///        Remote.\n    ///\n    ///        Remote's reported `newBalance` is its yield-only baseline (`_viewCheckBalance\n    ///        - bridgeAdjustment` post-subtract), which combined with Master's residual\n    ///        bridgeAdjustment gives consistent checkBalance across all orderings.\n    function _processSettlementAck(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        _markYieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n        uint256 newBalance = CrossChainV3Helper.decodeNewBalancePayload(\n            payload\n        );\n        bridgeAdjustment -= settlementSnapshot;\n        settlementSnapshot = 0;\n        remoteStrategyBalance = newBalance;\n        emit SettlementAcked(nonce, newBalance);\n        emit RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    function _processWithdrawRequestAck(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        _markYieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n        uint256 newBalance = CrossChainV3Helper.decodeNewBalancePayload(\n            payload\n        );\n        remoteStrategyBalance = newBalance;\n        // pendingWithdrawalAmount stays set \u2014 gates concurrent triggerClaim() calls\n        // until the leg-2 ack lands.\n        emit WithdrawRequestAcked(nonce, newBalance);\n        emit RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    function _processWithdrawClaimAck(\n        uint64 nonce,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes memory payload\n    ) internal {\n        _markYieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n        (\n            uint256 newBalance,\n            bool success,\n            uint256 ackAmount\n        ) = CrossChainV3Helper.decodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(payload);\n\n        if (success) {\n            // Tokens arrived alongside the ack. Forward what landed to the vault.\n            // `amount <= ackAmount` (not strict equality) so CCTP fast-finality fees\n            // are tolerated: the shortfall is the protocol fee, absorbed as yield drag\n            // and refreshed on the next BALANCE_CHECK. Mirrors the older\n            // `CrossChainMasterStrategy._onTokenReceived` which ignores `feeExecuted`\n            // entirely (marked `solhint-disable-next-line no-unused-vars`).\n            require(amount > 0, \"Master: claim ack missing tokens\");\n            require(amount <= ackAmount, \"Master: claim above ack\");\n            require(\n                amount <= pendingWithdrawalAmount,\n                \"Master: claim amount above pending\"\n            );\n            pendingWithdrawalAmount = 0;\n            // Forward delivered bridgeAsset to the vault.\n            uint256 bal = IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this));\n            if (bal > 0) {\n                IERC20(bridgeAsset).safeTransfer(vaultAddress, bal);\n                emit Withdrawal(bridgeAsset, bridgeAsset, bal);\n            }\n        }\n        // Either way, update remoteStrategyBalance to Remote's current view.\n        remoteStrategyBalance = newBalance;\n        emit WithdrawClaimAcked(nonce, newBalance, success);\n        emit RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    function _processYieldDepositAck(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        _markYieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n        uint256 newBalance = CrossChainV3Helper.decodeNewBalancePayload(\n            payload\n        );\n        remoteStrategyBalance = newBalance;\n        pendingAmount = 0;\n        emit DepositAcked(nonce, newBalance);\n        emit RemoteStrategyBalanceUpdated(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    // --- AbstractWOTokenStrategy hooks -------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _bridgeOutboundMsgType() internal pure override returns (uint32) {\n        return CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_OUT;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _preflightBridgeOutbound(uint256 amount) internal view override {\n        // Liquidity check: Remote's reported balance plus any unsettled bridge-channel\n        // delta must cover the bridge-out.\n        int256 available = int256(remoteStrategyBalance) + bridgeAdjustment;\n        require(\n            available >= int256(amount),\n            \"Master: insufficient remote liquidity\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _consumeOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount) internal override {\n        // Pull OToken from the user and burn it via the vault.\n        IERC20(oToken).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\n        IVault(vaultAddress).burnForStrategy(amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _deliverOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount, address recipient)\n        internal\n        override\n    {\n        IVault(vaultAddress).mintForStrategy(amount);\n        IERC20(oToken).safeTransfer(recipient, amount);\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0xa2f66ec17f3ffc41f0901cfad8fb42b28d4ca7def27137dd125c5bb2e2be8e4f","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/token/OUSD.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title OUSD Token Contract\n * @dev ERC20 compatible contract for OUSD\n * @dev Implements an elastic supply\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n */\nimport { IVault } from \"../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\nimport { Governable } from \"../governance/Governable.sol\";\nimport { SafeCast } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/math/SafeCast.sol\";\n\ncontract OUSD is Governable {\n    using SafeCast for int256;\n    using SafeCast for uint256;\n\n    /// @dev Event triggered when the supply changes\n    /// @param totalSupply Updated token total supply\n    /// @param rebasingCredits Updated token rebasing credits\n    /// @param rebasingCreditsPerToken Updated token rebasing credits per token\n    event TotalSupplyUpdatedHighres(\n        uint256 totalSupply,\n        uint256 rebasingCredits,\n        uint256 rebasingCreditsPerToken\n    );\n    /// @dev Event triggered when an account opts in for rebasing\n    /// @param account Address of the account\n    event AccountRebasingEnabled(address account);\n    /// @dev Event triggered when an account opts out of rebasing\n    /// @param account Address of the account\n    event AccountRebasingDisabled(address account);\n    /// @dev Emitted when `value` tokens are moved from one account `from` to\n    ///      another `to`.\n    /// @param from Address of the account tokens are moved from\n    /// @param to Address of the account tokens are moved to\n    /// @param value Amount of tokens transferred\n    event Transfer(address indexed from, address indexed to, uint256 value);\n    /// @dev Emitted when the allowance of a `spender` for an `owner` is set by\n    ///      a call to {approve}. `value` is the new allowance.\n    /// @param owner Address of the owner approving allowance\n    /// @param spender Address of the spender allowance is granted to\n    /// @param value Amount of tokens spender can transfer\n    event Approval(\n        address indexed owner,\n        address indexed spender,\n        uint256 value\n    );\n    /// @dev Yield resulting from {changeSupply} that a `source` account would\n    ///      receive is directed to `target` account.\n    /// @param source Address of the source forwarding the yield\n    /// @param target Address of the target receiving the yield\n    event YieldDelegated(address source, address target);\n    /// @dev Yield delegation from `source` account to the `target` account is\n    ///      suspended.\n    /// @param source Address of the source suspending yield forwarding\n    /// @param target Address of the target no longer receiving yield from `source`\n    ///        account\n    event YieldUndelegated(address source, address target);\n\n    enum RebaseOptions {\n        NotSet,\n
